;<span id="Recall_matrix">Recall matrix</span> (Recall[])
:the matrix that shows how recall depends on [[Glossary:Retrievability|retrievability (R)]]. In an ideal case, recall and [[Glossary:Retrievability|retrievability]] should be the same, however, there are factors that make it hard to achieve. For example, difficult [[Glossary:Item|items]] produce non-exponential [[Glossary:Forgetting_curve|forgetting curves]], SuperMemo finds it hard to sort [[Glossary:Item|items]] by [[Glossary:Difficulty|difficulty]], difficulty is fluid, etc. The '''Recall matrix''' is then used to predict recall on the basis of [[Glossary:Retrievability|retrievability]] for [[Glossary:Item|items]] with varying [[Glossary:Difficulty|difficulty]] and [[Glossary:Stability|stability]]. The matrix can be used to produce the approximation of the recall function which helps estimating recall even if no recall data is available. The '''Recall matrix''' can be compared to the forgetting curve matrix. In older algorithms, forgetting curve matrix was used with time measured in [[Glossary:U-Factor|U-Factor]] unit. The '''Recall matrix''' measures time in [[Glossary:Retrievability|retrievability]] units. You can inspect the '''Recall matrix''' using: '''[[Toolkit menu|Toolkit]] : [[Toolkit menu#Memory|Memory]] : [[Memory graphs (4D)|4D Graphs]] : Recall'''
